what_it_does: It is an implementation guide that lays out the concrete steps to add a 'Context Forager' UI layer to an existing canvas application: a new forager shape, a search bar, result chips/filters, in-canvas expansion, computed edges, a time backfill script, and a handoff of selected forager items into a Claude chat turn.
observations: It names specific files and line ranges: ForagerShape.tsx, NodeShape.tsx, App.tsx, ForagerBar.tsx, and flows/ts_backfill.py.
It defines five guiding principles: assembly not invention, reuse-don't-parallel, registry-is-truth, fail-loud, and spatial-frame-as-UI-identity.
The work is sequenced by priority labels: A1/A2 shape+loader, Search lane, B3 expand, B4 edges, C1 chips, C2 time flow, D1 handoff.
It calls out API routes such as /api/cognition_info, /api/corpus-query, and /api/claude/turn, and notes that a bridge route for query_corpus may need to be added.
It introduces a 'coexistence rule' requiring loadGraph's prune to filter by shape.type === 'node' and the forager clear to filter type === 'forager'.
It bumps the canvas persistenceKey to company-canvas-v4 because a new shape type is being introduced.
It ends with explicit 'Don'ts' and 'Preserves' to limit blast radius on existing graph editor, S1 panel, indicate/locus machinery, and routes.
standouts: The 'coexistence rule' that keeps forager circles and graph nodes on the same canvas without mutual interference during prune/clear.
The strong 'assembly, not invention' stance: reuse NodeShape, Edges overlay, AddressHelp/History panel, Workshop modal, and S1 panel patterns rather than building parallels.
The handoff contract to ClaudeChat: read selected forager shapes, build a SET BLOCK context_block, and send it on the next /api/claude/turn.
The registry-is-truth / fail-loud pairing: chips derive from real index fields, empties are honest, and the forager set is session-local view-side only.
conventions: Follows a reuse-don't-parallel pattern by mapping each new need to an existing component or API pattern.
Follows incremental persistence versioning by bumping the key when a new shape type is introduced.
Follows explicit constraint documentation through 'Don'ts' and 'Preserves' sections.
Breaks the 'proposes_only' expectation for flows/ts_backfill.py by intentionally writing corpus records to backfill ts_source.
Uses client-side derivation for chips and edges in v1 rather than waiting for a dedicated backend facets endpoint.
concerns: Specific line-number references (e.g., NodeShape.tsx:194-222, App.tsx:364) will drift as the codebase changes.
The /api/corpus-query route and bridge dispatch are described as research tasks, so the guide contains an unresolved integration gap.
C1 chips are client-derived from the loaded set in v1, which may not reflect true corpus facets and could produce inconsistent chip values.
D1 handoff relies on a registryStore hook or a window-level getter set in App, which is an ad-hoc selection-access mechanism.
flows/ts_backfill.py writes corpus records with a 'latest-seq-wins' rule; the file does not detail how collisions or rollback are handled.
The 're-hit = pulse class, no dupe' behavior is mentioned but not specified in terms of animation or state update.
notes: A buildable checklist for grafting a corpus-search overlay onto the canvas while keeping graph nodes untouched and preserving existing panel and route contracts.
questions: What is the 'Synthesis' document referenced in the principles?
What are the exact schemas and index fields of the corpus records that chips derive from?
What is the existing 'S1 panel' and 'turn shape' contract?
What is g23's extract_exchanges and what does g13 clusters.json contain?
Is /api/corpus-query already implemented, or does the bridge need a brand-new GET route and dispatch handler?
What does 'run:// graph stays backend-truth' mean in this codebase?
What is the 'proposes_only pattern' that ts_backfill.py is said to violate?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the file is titled an implementation guide and it performs that role, mapping feature requirements to specific file edits, API calls, and design constraints.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to add a canvas-based context foraging tool that lets users search corpus records, visualize the hits as forager circles alongside existing graph nodes, filter and explore them, and feed a user selection into a Claude chat turn as opaque context, all while reusing existing editor machinery and keeping the backend graph as the source of truth.
novelty: The notion of a second, non-graph shape type (forager circles) coexisting on the same canvas with its own vocabulary, prune rules, and handoff path to a conversational agent; the explicit persistence-key bump and 'coexistence rule' to avoid shape-type collisions; and a one-time backfill script that writes corpus records to add a ts_source field.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, file-level integration plan and a set of boundary constraints for adding corpus search/selection to the canvas without altering graph node props or backend graph truth; it identifies reusable UI patterns, flags anti-patterns, defines a selection-to-chat handoff contract, and records the need to version the canvas persistence key.
summary_for_embedding: Implementation guide for adding a 'Context Forager' shape and search UI to a tldraw-based canvas app, specifying file changes, API usage, coexistence rules with graph nodes, chip/filter behavior, a time-backfill script, and handoff of selected context to a Claude chat turn.
intention_for_embedding: To define a buildable, minimally invasive integration of corpus context retrieval and selection into the existing canvas editor and Claude chat flow.
